title: Establishing the Task Force To Eliminate Fraud
summary: Executive Order 14395, issued on March 16, 2026, establishes the Task Force To Eliminate Fraud, aiming to combat widespread fraud, waste, and abuse in federally funded benefit programs administered by states. The order highlights that some states exploit loopholes, avoid eligibility validation, and refuse to implement basic fraud controls, leading to exploitation by various entities including illegal aliens, criminals, and foreign gangs. The document cites significant fraud in Minnesota, including Medicaid and food stamp fraud, a $250 million scam involving feeding programs for children, and the theft of federal childcare funds by an organized ring. The Task Force, chaired by the Vice President, will coordinate a national strategy to improve eligibility verification, implement pre-payment controls, identify fraud indicators, facilitate information sharing between federal and state entities, disrupt fraud networks, and audit compliance. Agencies are required to identify high-risk transactions, develop anti-fraud requirements, and submit implementation plans. The Attorney General is directed to promote private civil actions to recover funds lost to fraud.
